content.full_text:
APR 9 I 49FM'90

Senator David Roberti
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE

April 5, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street          Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I am requesting a formal written opinion from the FPPC to ascertain whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator David Roberti may receive contributions from campaign committees controlled by other officeholders or candidates for elective office.

It is my understanding that the FPPC has instructed staff to develop regulations to determine when expenditure activities of a ballot measure committee are deemed to so benefit a candidate that the expenditures become contributions to the candidate.

It is also our understanding that the Commission has already instructed the FPPC staff to modify the Hiltachk Advice Letter to allow candidates to transfer funds to another candidate's controlled ballot measure committee.

Senator Roberti controls the following ballot measure committees:

1. House the Homeless, A Committee for Proposition 107:
   Senator David Roberti, Chair               I.D. 891049

2. Californians Against Reapportionment Dishonesty:
   David Roberti, Chair                       I.D. 890905

3. Senator David Roberti Ballot Measure Committee    I.D. 892004

Your prompt response to this request would be appreciated.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Shirley Wechsler, Treasurer

sw

Campaign Finance Office • 11783 Laurelcrest Drive • Studio City, California 91604 • (213) 877-5835
NOT PRINTED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE

sections.question: Whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator David Roberti may receive contributions from campaign committees controlled by other officeholders or candidates for elective office.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator Roberti can accept contributions from other officeholders' or candidates' campaign committees, and references FPPC staff work on regulations regarding when ballot measure expenditures benefit candidates enough to constitute contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or formal opinion section present. Document references three ballot measure committees controlled by Senator Roberti and mentions prior FPPC work on related regulations and advice letters.
